pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, much like my heart. He is gone, and with him, the very light of the world has been extinguished. I find myself wandering these halls, a ghost before I am even dead, feeling as though my soul has become too heavy for this mortal coil to bear. Is there not a certain grace in simply fading away? To slip quietly into the shadows and let the night reclaim what remains of me?
